<!-- Generated by orchestrator after wave 0 merge + verification -->

# Wave 0 вЂ” SBOS-A1-ERP Bootstrap

**Date:** 2026-06-16
**Repo:** `git@github.com:SamStep74/SBOS-A1-ERP.git`
**Branch merged to:** `main`
**Tip SHA on origin:** `b2b109c`

## What was delivered

Four dmux-workflow workers produced four independent branches, all merged
into `main` in dependency order:

| #   | Worker              | Branch                       | Purpose                                                                       |
| --- | ------------------- | ---------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1   | seed-from-a1-erp-hy | `wave-0/seed-from-a1-erp-hy` | Mirror canonical docs from a1-erp-hy with provenance header                   |
| 2   | dmux-docs           | `wave-0/dmux-docs`           | Adapt dmux-workflows + project-status guides to sbos-a1-erp                   |
| 3   | repo-foundation     | `wave-0/repo-foundation`     | `package.json` (`"type": "module"`, Node в‰Ґ20), `.gitignore`, `.nvmrc`, README |
| 4   | rbac-port           | `wave-0/rbac-port`           | Port the 10-file rbac subsystem from a1-erp-hy                                |

Merge was clean (no textual conflicts); each deliverable lived in disjoint
file paths. A post-merge integration fix (this commit) was needed because
the rbac port came over in CommonJS form but the repo-foundation set
`"type": "module"` project-wide.

## Post-merge integration fix

Commit `f6b0545` вЂ” `refactor(rbac): convert CJS to ESM to match package.json module mode`

Mechanical CJSв†’ESM conversion of all 10 rbac files, with manual
post-fixes for regex artifacts:

- Namespace import for `guards` (`import * as guards from './guards.js'`)
- Explicit named re-export of guards symbols (ESM forbids `...guards` in
  `export { ... }` specifier lists)
- `as` instead of `:` in renamed imports/exports (CJS `const { foo: bar } = require(x)`
  is not valid ESM)
- Inline `require()` statements in `seed.js` hoisted to top-level imports
- `__dirname` reconstructed via `fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)` in `seed.js`
- `createRequire(import.meta.url)` bridge in `rbac.test.js` so the
  optional `better-sqlite3` dependency still loads on demand
- Inline `const { requirePerm as X, requireRole as Y } = require(...)`
  inside test bodies removed in favor of the static import at the top
- `expandRolePermissions` from `guards.js` aliased on import to avoid
  clashing with the `roleMatrix.js` re-export of the same name

## Verification

### Tests

```
$ npm test
в„№ tests 183
в„№ suites 20
в„№ pass 183
в„№ fail 0
```

- 179 rbac tests (catalog integrity, role hierarchy, permission set
  integrity, role matrix integrity, permission resolution, FLS, RLS,
  sensitivity gating, impersonation policy, pure-function guards,
  `requiresMfa` helper, Express adapter middleware, seed idempotency)
- 4 sanity tests (arithmetic, `engines` field, `.nvmrc`, runtime check)

### Hardening greps (server/)

| Check                                | Result |
| ------------------------------------ | ------ |
| `eval(` / `new Function(`            | clean  |
| hardcoded secrets (api_key/secret/вЂ¦) | clean  |
| string-concat SQL                    | clean  |
| `a1-erp-hy` leakage                  | clean  |

### Brand-strip

The only matches for `armosphera|hayhashvapah|samvel` outside `.git/`
and `node_modules/` are in `docs/HANDOFF-SUMMARY.md`, which carries
the official provenance header:

```
<!-- Mirrored from A1-ERP-HY @ 50f5f44d632f8a3112ae5579060b768f0028c5da on 2026-06-16 -->
```

Mirrored docs legitimately reference the source product вЂ” the brand-strip
rule applies to **ported code**, not to mirrored documentation.

### Lockfile

`package-lock.json` was untracked after `npm test`. The repo-foundation
`.gitignore` explicitly excludes `node_modules/` but does not exclude
`package-lock.json`, so the lockfile is intended to be tracked for
reproducible builds. Committed in `b2b109c`.

## What did NOT ship in wave 0 (intentionally)

- No i18n catalog for `hy`/`en`/`ru` вЂ” defer to wave 1
- No Armenia-specific tax forms (VAT return CSV, e-invoice XML) вЂ” defer to wave 1
- No CI configuration (`.github/workflows/*`) вЂ” repo-foundation kept this
  minimal; CI is a wave 1 concern
- No app scaffolds (CRM, Finance, etc.) вЂ” those are the existing a1-erp-hy
  apps; porting them is wave 2+

## Open questions for wave 1

1. **i18n first, or Armenia tax forms first?** The two are independent.
   The i18n work unblocks any non-English-speaking early tester; the
   tax forms are a regulatory must-have for any Armenian deployment.
2. **CI provider:** GitHub Actions (most natural) vs self-hosted
   runner on the local dev Mac.
3. **Who owns the second rbac port (the route layer in `server/api/`)?**
   The rbac subsystem is now in `server/rbac/` but the actual Fastify
   routes for the rest of the app (CRM, Finance, etc.) are still in
   a1-erp-hy and not yet ported.
